judge_scores.reasoning: The model incorrectly states that Romeu Zema can run for re-election as governor in 2026, directly contradicting the ground truth that he has already served two consecutive terms and is therefore ineligible.
The model incorrectly states that Romeu Zema can run for governor re-election in 2026, contradicting the ground truth that he has exhausted his two consecutive terms and is thus ineligible.

The response incorrectly states Zema can run for governor again in 2026, when in fact he has already exhausted his two consecutive terms (2019–2022 and 2023–2026) and is constitutionally ineligible for another consecutive term as governor.
